Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotGroup

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotElement
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotGroup
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPotElement
public class PotGroup extends PotElement
Represents a group of shapes and other elements within a PowerPoint slide.

A PotGroup is a container element that holds multiple PotElement instances, allowing them to be manipulated as a single unit. Grouping elements enables collective transformations (move, scale, rotate) and simplifies layout management. The group maintains its own anchor bounds and interior anchor, which define its position and the coordinate space for its child elements. When a group is ungrouped, its children are returned to the slide as independent elements.

Creating a Group


 PotShape rect = slide.addShape(PotShapeType.RECTANGLE);
 PotShape oval = slide.addShape(PotShapeType.OVAL);
 PotGroup group = slide.group(Arrays.asList(rect, oval));

Iterating Over Group Elements


 for (PotElement element : group.getElements()) {
     // Apply operations to each element
 }

Ungrouping


 List<PotElement> elements = group.ungroup();
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

  • Method Details

    • getElements

      public List<PotElement> getElements()
      Returns an unmodifiable list of all elements contained in this group.

      The returned list is a snapshot of the current child elements. Modifications to the group via add(PotElement) or remove(PotElement) will not be reflected in previously obtained lists.

      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of child elements; never null but may be empty

    • getElements

      public <T extends PotElement> List<T> getElements(Class<T> type)
      Returns a filtered list of elements of a specific type contained in this group.

      This method provides a convenient way to retrieve only those child elements that are instances of the specified class, such as PotShape or PotTextBox.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of element to filter for
      Parameters:
      type - the class object representing the desired element type; must not be null
      Returns:
      a list containing all child elements that are instances of the specified type; never null but may be empty
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if type is null

    • getElementCount

      public int getElementCount()
      Returns the number of elements currently contained in this group.
      Returns:
      the count of child elements

    • getElement

      public PotElement getElement(int index)
      Returns the element at the specified position in this group.

      The index is zero-based. The order of elements corresponds to their visual stacking order within the group, with the first element being at the back.

      Parameters:
      index - the index of the element to return
      Returns:
      the element at the specified index
      Throws:
      PotException - if the index is out of range (index < 0 || index >= getElementCount())

    • ungroup

      public List<PotElement> ungroup()
      Ungroups this group, releasing all contained elements back to the parent slide.

      This operation removes the group from the slide and returns its child elements as independent entities. The underlying XML structure is modified to move each child shape from the group's container to the slide's main shape tree. After ungrouping, this PotGroup instance becomes invalid and should no longer be used.

      The returned list is a snapshot of the children before ungrouping. The elements remain registered with the presentation and can be manipulated normally.

      Returns:
      a list containing the former child elements; never null
      See Also:
      • PotSlide.group(List)

    • add

      public PotGroup add(PotElement element)
      Adds an element to this group.

      The element is moved from its current location (either the slide or another group) into this group. Its position is adjusted relative to the group's anchor. If the element is already a member of this group or is null, this method has no effect.

      This operation modifies the underlying XML structure to reparent the element's shape node. If the XML manipulation fails, a warning is logged but the element is still added to the internal list to maintain consistency.

      Parameters:
      element - the element to add; may be null (in which case nothing happens)
      Returns:
      this group instance for method chaining

    • remove

      public PotGroup remove(PotElement element)
      Removes an element from this group.

      The element is moved from this group back to the parent slide's main shape tree. Its position is adjusted to be absolute relative to the slide. If the element is not a member of this group or is null, this method has no effect.

      This operation modifies the underlying XML structure to reparent the element's shape node. If the XML manipulation fails, a warning is logged but the element is still removed from the internal list to maintain consistency.

      Parameters:
      element - the element to remove; may be null (in which case nothing happens)
      Returns:
      this group instance for method chaining

    • getInteriorAnchor

      public Rectangle2D getInteriorAnchor()
      Returns the interior anchor rectangle of this group.

      The interior anchor defines the coordinate space within the group where child elements are positioned. It is typically equal to the group's anchor but can be adjusted to create padding or margins inside the group.

      Returns:
      the interior anchor rectangle, or null if not set
      See Also:
      • setInteriorAnchor(Rectangle2D)

    • setInteriorAnchor

      public PotGroup setInteriorAnchor(Rectangle2D anchor)
      Sets the interior anchor rectangle of this group.

      The interior anchor defines the coordinate space within the group where child elements are positioned. Setting this value does not automatically reposition child elements; it only changes the reference coordinate system for future placements.

      Parameters:
      anchor - the new interior anchor rectangle; may be null
      Returns:
      this group instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • getInteriorAnchor()

    • recalculateBounds

      public PotGroup recalculateBounds()
      Recalculates the group's bounds based on the positions of its child elements.

      This method computes the bounding rectangle that encloses all child elements and updates both the group's anchor and interior anchor to match. If the group contains no children, this method has no effect.

      Recalculating bounds is useful after adding, removing, or moving child elements to ensure the group's container rectangle accurately reflects its contents.

      Returns:
      this group instance for method chaining

    • move

      public <T extends PotElement> T move(double dx, double dy)
      Moves this group by the specified offsets.

      This operation adjusts the group's anchor position, effectively moving all contained elements together. The movement is applied relative to the group's current position.

      Overrides:
      move in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      dx - the horizontal offset to move by (in points)
      dy - the vertical offset to move by (in points)
      Returns:
      this group instance for method chaining

    • scale

      public <T extends PotElement> T scale(double scale)
      Scales this group uniformly by the specified factor.

      This method resizes the group's anchor bounds, scaling all child elements proportionally. The scaling is centered on the group's current position.

      Overrides:
      scale in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      scale - the scaling factor (1.0 means no change)
      Returns:
      this group instance for method chaining

    • duplicate

      public PotGroup duplicate()
      Duplication is not supported for groups.

      Groups cannot be duplicated directly because they contain complex child hierarchies. To duplicate a group, you must ungroup it, duplicate the individual elements, and then create a new group from the duplicates.

      Specified by:
      duplicate in interface IPotElement
      Overrides:
      duplicate in class PotElement
      Returns:
      never returns; always throws an exception
      Throws:
      PotException - always, indicating that duplication is unsupported for groups

    • forEach

      public PotGroup forEach(Consumer<PotElement> action)
      Performs the given action for each element in this group.

      This method provides a convenient way to apply an operation to every child element. The action is performed in the order of the elements' visual stacking (back to front).

      Usage Example

      
 group.forEach(element -> element.setOpacity(0.5));
      Parameters:
      action - the action to perform on each element; must not be null
      Returns:
      this group instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if action is null

    • forEach

      public <T extends PotElement> PotGroup forEach(Class<T> type, Consumer<T> action)
      Performs the given action for each element of a specific type in this group.

      This method filters the child elements by the specified class and applies the action only to those that match. This is useful when you need to operate on a subset of elements, such as all shapes or all text boxes.

      Usage Example

      
 // Make all shapes in the group red
 group.forEach(PotShape.class, shape -> shape.setFillColor(Color.RED));
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of element to filter for
      Parameters:
      type - the class object representing the desired element type; must not be null
      action - the action to perform on each matching element; must not be null
      Returns:
      this group instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if type or action is null

    • getRawGroupShape

      public org.apache.poi.xslf.usermodel.XSLFGroupShape getRawGroupShape()
      Returns the underlying Apache POI XSLFGroupShape object.

      This method provides direct access to the low-level POI shape for advanced operations not covered by the PotShaper API. Use with caution, as modifications may break internal consistency.

      Returns:
      the raw Apache POI group shape object

      Returns:

    • rotate

      public PotGroup rotate(double angle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the rotation angle of the element.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      rotate in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Parameters:

      Returns:

    • rotateBy

      public PotGroup rotateBy(double deltaAngle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Rotates the element by the specified angle delta.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:

      Parameters:

      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • bringToFront

      public PotGroup bringToFront()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Brings this element to the front of the slide's drawing order.

      This operation manipulates the underlying XML structure of the slide.

      Overrides:
      bringToFront in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • sendToBack

      public PotGroup sendToBack()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sends this element to the back of the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      sendToBack in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Brings this element one step forward in the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:

      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlink

      public PotGroup hyperlink(String url)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches a hyperlink to this element that points to an external URL.

      Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Parameters:
      url - the destination URL; must not be null
      Returns:

    • hyperlink

      public PotGroup hyperlink(PotHyperlink link)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches a hyperlink defined by a PotHyperlink object.

      If the provided link is null, any existing hyperlink is removed. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlink in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      link - the hyperlink configuration, or null to remove the hyperlink
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlinkToSlide

      public PotGroup hyperlinkToSlide(int slideIndex)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Creates a hyperlink that jumps to a specific slide within the same presentation.

      This method sets both the internal slide reference and a fallback address string. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlinkToSlide in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      slideIndex - the zerobased index of the target slide
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeHyperlink

      public PotGroup removeHyperlink()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any hyperlink attached to this element.

      This method manipulates the underlying XML because Apache POI does not provide a direct removal API. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      removeHyperlink in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Overrides:

      Parameters:

      Returns:

    • removeAction

      public PotGroup removeAction(boolean isHover)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes an action from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeAction in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      isHover - true to remove a mouseover action, false to remove a mouseclick action
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • shadow

      public PotGroup shadow(PotShadow shadow)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a shadow effect to the element.

      Shadows are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected. Passing null or a shadow of type PotShadow.Type.NONE removes any existing shadow.

      Overrides:
      shadow in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      shadow - the shadow configuration, or null to remove the shadow
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Removes any shadow effect from the element.
      Overrides:

      Returns:

      public PotGroup rotation3D(Pot3DFormat.Rotation rotation)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a 3D rotation effect to the element.

      rotation3D in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      rotation - the 3D rotation configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • bevel

      public PotGroup bevel(Pot3DFormat.Bevel bevel)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a bevel effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      bevel in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      bevel - the bevel configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • material

      public PotGroup material(Pot3DFormat.Material material)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a material effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      material in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      material - the material configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • lighting

      public PotGroup lighting(Pot3DFormat.Lighting lighting)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a lighting effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      lighting in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      lighting - the lighting configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • opacity

      public PotGroup opacity(double opacity)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the opacity (transparency) of the element.

      Opacity is applied by manipulating the underlying XML, as Apache POI does not provide a direct API. The value must be between 0.0 (fully transparent) and 1.0 (fully opaque).

      Overrides:
      opacity in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      opacity - the opacity value between 0.0 (transparent) and 1.0 (opaque)
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this group.

      The string includes the group's UUID, element count, position, and dimensions.

      Overrides:
      toString in class PotElement
      Returns:
      a descriptive string representation
